seoTitle: How to Create a PayPal Account Without Your Phone (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to sign up for PayPal while protecting your personal number, using a private virtual line to complete required SMS verification.
intro: Financial compliance rules mean PayPal always requires some form of text verification, so you can't skip phone authentication entirely. This guide shows how to register without exposing your personal mobile number by using a private virtual number for the SMS step. It's aimed at privacy-conscious users setting up a new account.

steps.heading: Start registration
steps.body: Go to PayPal's sign-up page, click Sign Up, and choose Personal Account, then select your country to load the identity form.

steps.heading: Get a private virtual number
steps.body: Open a separate tab, access a reputable virtual SMS provider, and lease a dedicated phone line, avoiding low-tier or free public options that often fail security checks.

steps.heading: Enter and verify the number
steps.body: Paste the temporary number into PayPal's mobile field, trigger the SMS, then check your virtual number console for the incoming 6-digit code.

steps.heading: Complete your profile
steps.body: Type the verification code into PayPal, then fill in your full legal name, address, and a strong password.

steps.heading: Confirm your email
steps.body: Check your inbox for PayPal's security alert and click Confirm Email Address to finish activating your account.

faq.question: Can I use a free public SMS receiving site?
faq.answer: Free public SMS portals and low-tier VoIP numbers often fail PayPal's security checks and can trigger automatic account lockouts.

faq.question: Is skipping phone verification entirely possible?
faq.answer: No, PayPal's compliance rules require an active phone verification step for every new account.

faq.question: Will using a virtual number affect account trust later?
faq.answer: As long as the number stays reachable if PayPal ever needs to re-verify, it typically doesn't cause ongoing issues.

faq.question: What should I do if I lose access to my virtual number later?
faq.answer: Confirming your email address gives you an alternative recovery path in case you can't access the original verification number again.
